steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The word 'Yet' signals a surprising contrast: despite the Navajo-Churro's reputation for coarseness compared to the Merino's softness, something unexpected is true. The correct choice must capture that contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘the Navajo-Churro more closely resembles its ancestor, the mouflon, in the uniform softness of its inner fiber, while the Merino more closely resembles the mouflon in the variable diameter of its outer fiber’: Erases the contrast rather than capturing it. The passage distinguishes outer and inner fibers. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘domestication resulted in a counterintuitive increase in the inner fiber's minimum diameter, making the inner fiber of the Merino and the Navajo-Churro less suitable for delicate garments than the mouflon's inner fiber is’: A diameter increase would mean coarser fiber, which does not create the surprising softness the contrast requires. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘the domestication of the mouflon and the subsequent selection process that produced the Merino and the Navajo-Churro resulted in greater softness of outer and inner fiber alike’: Too broad and not supported; the contrast is specific to inner fiber at its softest range, not the breed overall. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘the selection process that enabled the Navajo-Churro to retain its somewhat coarse outer fiber also resulted in inner fiber that, at its softest, is softer than either the mouflon's or the Merino's inner fiber’: Maintains the coarseness of the outer layer while introducing the genuinely surprising fact that the inner fiber can surpass even the Merino in softness. That is the unexpected contrast 'Yet' introduces. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
